title: Tom Colicchio’s Final Service
description: "Through hosting two decades of Top Chef, writing four books, and opening restaurants across the country, the celebrity chef never stopped showing up to cook in the kitchen of Craft, his pioneering New York restaurant. Until he decided to close it."
hook: Tom Colicchio spent twenty years on TV while his flagship restaurant quietly became a relic. This is about why he finally let it go.

title: Saint Peter: Hujar’s Contact Sheets at the Morgan
description: “The story that unfolds among the contact sheets is sometimes quite funny (Hujar at the dentist in a houndstooth sweater).”
hook: Peter Hujar's contact sheets show a life lived behind the camera—funny, tender, obsessive. A photographer's secret self-portrait.

title: “That Guy is Still Out There”
description: "Five years after Anthony Broadwater was belatedly cleared for the sexual assault of Alice Sebold, the questions of how he came to be wrongly convicted and how one or more serial rapists operated for years with little consequence have only deepened."
hook: Alice Sebold's exoneree and a serial rapist still at large: the case that exposes how justice systems fail women twice over.

title: The Prisoner didn't just predict the next 60 years of TV—it predicted the next 60 years, period
description: The psychedelic British spy-fi series hits Criterion Channel at a moment of peak relevancy.
hook: A 1960s psychedelic nightmare about total surveillance feels less like history and more like prophecy right now.

title: Nirvanna the Band the Show the Movie the Article
description: Beyond the ever-growing title, the theatrical outing for cult comedy duo Nirvanna The Band is a metatextual triumph – we explore the rich pop culture references, cinephilia and absurdity that led to this inevitably layered movie outing.
hook: A cult comedy duo's movie is a three-layer cake of pop culture obsession, cinephilia, and meta-absurdity that actually earns the bit.
